text: Figure 1. Schematic illustration of preparation and anti-SAP mechanisms of Ab-Fc-EL-EV@Que. (A) Preparation process of Ab-Fc-EL-EV@Que. (B) The anti-SAP mechanisms of Ab-Fc-EL-EV@Que. The high concentration of extracellular Prdx1 in local pancreas recruited Ab-Fc-EL-EV@Que actively. After pancreas targeting, Ab-Fc-EL-EV@Que released anti-Prdx1 antibodies under high concentration of elastase in SAP microenvironment. The antibodies neutralized extracellular Prdx1 and thereby inhibited macrophage-mediated inflammation via TLR3/NF-kB signaling pathway. Meanwhile, CD63-overexpressed EV@Que entered acinar cells and released Que to promote intracellular Prdx1 acetylation, enhancing antioxidant capacity of acinar cells. Therefore, through targeted treatment of internal and external Prdx1, this preparation can effectively alleviate the inflammatory microenvironment of SAP and reduce acinar cell death.
